---
title: Cell Image Library
alias: &ALIAS cellimagelibrary
href: http://www.cellimagelibrary.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Cell Image Library (CIL) is a public repository for images, videos, and animations of cells, providing open access to microscopy data for cell biology research.

## Key Features

- Hosts thousands of high-resolution light and electron microscopy images of diverse cell types
- Supports multiple imaging modalities including fluorescence, DIC, confocal, and EM
- Provides standardized metadata describing cell type, staining, imaging conditions, and provenance
- Enables download of raw image data in standard formats for computational analysis
- Curated and maintained to ensure data quality and long-term accessibility

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing reference cell morphology images for training image segmentation and classification models
- Studying morphological changes in immune cells during pathogen challenge
- Generating training data for AI-driven phenotypic screening of antimicrobial compounds
- Comparing cellular phenotypes across infection conditions using publicly available microscopy data
- Integrating CIL images with molecular data for multi-modal cell biology studies

## Getting Started

Visit [Cell Image Library](http://www.cellimagelibrary.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
